overview: Mono-white voltron-token engine that wins by attacking with Giada to generate a resilient army, then leveraging her tap ability to pump her for lethal or using tokens to control the board and out-value opponents. Excels in midrange and control matchups where it can stabilize and snowball, but must race fast aggro and artifact/ramp decks.
win_conditions: Giada with a full board of tapped tokens for massive +1/+1 counters, or a fully equipped Giada.
The goal is to swing with Giada and enough tokens to either go lethal or leave the opponent at 0 life after combat.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably fetchable or ramp-able), Sol Ring or other mana acceleration, and either Giada or a protective/early threat.
Keep hands with removal or bounce if facing aggro/control.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, no ramp, or only removal with no threats/land.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, drop Sol Ring or other ramp.
Turn 2: Cast Giada if possible, or play a cheap land/ramp to set up for Turn 3 Giada.
If you can't cast Giada early, use ramp to accelerate and play cheap white creatures or equipment to stabilize.
Prioritize playing lands to ensure you can cast Giada by Turn 3.
mid_game: Mana should be hoarded for Giada's pump ability or key removal.
Don't overcommit to attacking if you can't pump Giada or block effectively.
Use tokens as a resource: tap them to pump Giada, or leave them untapped to block/attack.
Graveyard is generally less relevant unless using bounce or recursion
Commit threats when you have a clear path to lethal or when you can stabilize against an aggressive board.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or can easily remove Giada.
Use tokens to trade favorably and preserve Giada's life total.
late_game: Giada with a full board of tapped tokens for massive +1/+1 counters, or a fully equipped Giada.
The goal is to swing with Giada and enough tokens to either go lethal or leave the opponent at 0 life after combat.
Go all-in when you have enough tapped creatures to pump Giada for lethal, or when the opponent is low enough that a single combat step will win.
Also commit when you can safely ignore their removal (e.g., they have no instant-speed interaction).

sequencing_tips: Common mistake: Attacking with Giada without tapping creatures to pump her, leaving her vulnerable to removal or unable to close the game.
Always calculate pump math before committing.
Another mistake: Overcommitting to the board without considering sweepers.
Use tokens as a buffer, not just for damage.
Giada's lifelink is crucial for stabilizing against aggressive decks, so don't neglect blocking.
Equipment synergizes well but isn't strictly necessary

matchups.advice: Use mana dorks/ramp to cast Giada on turn 2 or 3 before they can comfortably counter. Use cheap, efficient threats or equipment to pressure. If they counter, use bounce or protection to recast.
Apply early pressure with tokens and Giada. Use artifact hate and removal to slow their acceleration. Don't let them get too far ahead; trade efficiently and use lifegain to outlast them.
Stabilize with early removal, bounce, and board wipes. Use tokens to block and trade favorably. Prioritize keeping Giada alive and use her lifelink to stabilize life total.

Pressure with consistent attacks and token generation. Use card draw to out-value them. Force them to use removal on Giada, then swing with tokens. If they sweep, rebuild quickly with ramp and cheap threats.
Value trades carefully. Use your tokens to out-value theirs. Prioritize keeping Giada alive. Use removal on their Giada or key pump sources. The deck with better card advantage and removal wins.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes (Krenko's tokens are vulnerable), artifact acceleration (Blech), and combo win conditions.
Key tells: Opponent tapping out on non-creature spells often signals a sweeper or combo piece.
Opponent keeping mana open on their turn end suggests a counter or instant-speed interaction.
